diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with intrauterine growth deficiency, microcephaly, alopecia, bilateral microtia with canal atresia, conductive hearing loss, partial left facial palsy, posterior cleft palate, left choanal stenosis, tetralogy of Fallot, developmental delay, and right thumb polydactyly. These features are consistent with Johnson–McMillin Syndrome, a neuroectodermal syndrome with conductive hearing loss and microtia. The diagnosis was supported by the similarity of the patient's features to those previously reported in cases of Johnson–McMillin Syndrome, including alopecia, microtia, and conductive hearing loss.
